name: Halifax
tagline: Maritime Charm, Ocean Views, and Canada's Most Affordable Coast
description: Halifax is the cultural and economic hub of Atlantic Canada, offering a walkable waterfront downtown, a thriving food scene built on fresh seafood, and housing costs far below Toronto or Vancouver. Universal healthcare, a strong sense of community, and proximity to stunning Nova Scotian coastline make Halifax an underrated FIRE destination for those who enjoy maritime living and four-season outdoor recreation.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for Canadian citizens. Others: Super Visa (for parents/grandparents, up to 5 years) or Visitor Record extensions. No dedicated retirement visa — Canada lacks a retiree-specific immigration pathway.
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – one of Canada's safest cities
quickFacts.climate: Humid continental with cold, snowy winters and warm summers; ocean-moderated

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Clayton Park, Fairview, or Dartmouth (across the harbor)

dining.description: Fish and chips spots, pub nights, and casual dining 2x/week; mostly cooking at home

healthcare.description: Universal healthcare covers most costs for residents; budget for dental, vision, prescriptions

transportation.description: Halifax Transit bus pass or a paid-off car; the city is walkable downtown

entertainment.description: Waterfront walks, Point Pleasant Park, free festivals, library; occasional live music

1M.lifestyle: Halifax is one of the more comfortable cities at this budget, largely because universal healthcare eliminates a major expense. A one-bedroom in Clayton Park, Fairview, or Dartmouth is affordable, and the waterfront is free to enjoy year-round. You will cook most meals at home with occasional pub nights and fish-and-chips outings. Winters are cold and snowy with short days, which is the main lifestyle tradeoff.

housing.description: 2BR apartment on the Halifax waterfront or a townhome in the South End

dining.description: Regular dining at waterfront restaurants, lobster dinners, Halifax brewery scene

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus private supplemental for dental, vision, massage, prescriptions

transportation.description: Reliable car for exploring Nova Scotia, winter tires, full insurance

entertainment.description: Neptune Theatre season pass, sailing lessons, live music on Argyle Street, gym membership

2M.lifestyle: A waterfront apartment or a South End townhome, regular dining at local restaurants including fresh lobster, and a reliable car for weekend drives to Peggy's Cove or along the Cabot Trail. Universal healthcare plus private supplemental for dental and vision keeps medical costs low. The Halifax brewery and live music scene provide regular entertainment. The budget is comfortable here with room to spare.

housing.description: 3BR heritage home in the South End or a waterfront property in Bedford or Eastern Passage

dining.description: Fine dining at Stories, Bar Kismet, and Brooklyn Warehouse; regular seafood feasts

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus comprehensive private insurance for enhanced care and faster access

transportation.description: Late-model AWD vehicle, premium insurance, ferry pass for harbor crossings

entertainment.description: Sailing club membership, cottage weekends, art gallery patronage, ski trips to Martock

3M.lifestyle: A heritage home in the South End or a waterfront property in Bedford, regular dining at restaurants like Stories and Bar Kismet, and a sailing club membership on the harbor. A good AWD vehicle for Nova Scotia road trips and weekend cottage getaways on the South Shore. Universal healthcare keeps medical costs minimal, and the margin at this level is substantial for a city of this size.

housing.description: Luxury waterfront home in Northwest Arm or a custom build in Bedford with ocean views

dining.description: Private chef for weekly dinners, fine dining multiple nights, wine club memberships

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus executive health programs and premium private supplements

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, second vehicle, flights from YHZ to Toronto or international destinations

entertainment.description: Royal Nova Scotia Yacht Squadron membership, arts patronage, private sailing, golf club

domesticHelp.description: Weekly house cleaning, snow removal, landscaping, property maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Cottage on the South Shore, quarterly international travel, boat ownership, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A waterfront home on the Northwest Arm, Royal Nova Scotia Yacht Squadron membership, a cottage on the South Shore, and a nice car. Regular flights from Halifax to Toronto or international destinations, fine dining multiple nights a week, and universal healthcare keeping medical costs negligible. The budget significantly exceeds what Halifax's cost of living requires, leaving a large margin for travel or savings.

housing.description: Estate property on the Northwest Arm or waterfront compound in Chester or Mahone Bay

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, exclusive dining events, catered parties with Atlantic seafood

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus top private supplements, executive wellness, medical travel if needed

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private car service, chartered flights, yacht

entertainment.description: Major arts and maritime philanthropy, international travel, private sailing regattas

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate manager, housekeeper, groundskeeper, personal assistant, boat captain

luxuryExtras.description: Multiple properties, first-class international travel, yacht, art collecting, community foundation

10M.lifestyle: An estate on the Northwest Arm or a waterfront compound in Chester or Mahone Bay, a cook who comes in several times a week, regular housekeeping, and first-class international travel. A boat for Atlantic coast cruising and comprehensive private supplements on top of universal healthcare. Halifax is a small city with limited luxury infrastructure, so much of this budget will go unspent. The appeal here is the maritime setting, tight community, and very low stress rather than access to high-end services.
